message:
fix(ci): capture grep matches to var instead of piping to head (#45)

Critical bug in the #45 regression guard I added in 6a29944:

    if strings "$bin" | grep -E '...' | head -5; then ...

`head -5` always exits 0 regardless of whether grep matched anything,
so without `set -o pipefail` the `if` condition was constant-true.
This meant the guard ALWAYS fired, blocking the v0.2.9 release with
a false positive even though the source fix in 15dc02e is working
correctly (verified by building locally and scanning the output —
no matches on a clean binary).

Fix: capture grep output into a variable with `|| true` to survive
`set -e` on no-match, then check `[ -n "$matches" ]`. The var-based
form has correct exit semantics without depending on pipefail.

Verified locally: fresh `bun build --compile` with the current source
produces no match (clean binary passes), and a synthetic broken input
matches correctly (regression is still caught).

Without this fix, every release would fail the guard forever. This
unblocks v0.2.9 so the user-facing #45 fix can actually ship.

files.patch:
@@ -75,9 +75,15 @@ jobs:
           # "HOT STOVE" banner). This is tight enough to avoid false positives
           # from legitimate JavaScript Unicode regex patterns like
           # `/[\u{D800}-\u{DFFF}]/u` that survive bundling inside JS source.
+          #
+          # Capture matches into a variable instead of using `if pipe | head`
+          # — `head -5` always exits 0 regardless of grep's match status, so
+          # the naive `if` form is a constant-true guard.
           for bin in dist/cass-memory-linux-x64 dist/cass-memory-windows-x64.exe; do
             echo "Scanning $bin for mangled trauma-guard emoji..."
-            if strings "$bin" | grep -E '\\u\{[0-9a-fA-F]+\} ?HOT STOVE|HOT STOVE ?\\u\{[0-9a-fA-F]+\}' | head -5; then
+            matches="$(strings "$bin" | grep -E '\\u\{[0-9a-fA-F]+\} ?HOT STOVE|HOT STOVE ?\\u\{[0-9a-fA-F]+\}' | head -5 || true)"
+            if [ -n "$matches" ]; then
+              echo "$matches"
               echo "::error::Binary $bin contains Rust-style \\u{...} escapes in trauma-guard text (regression of #45)."
               echo "::error::A String.raw template almost certainly has a literal non-ASCII character."
               echo "::error::Route the character through \${VAR} interpolation. See src/trauma_guard_script.ts."
@@ -140,7 +146,9 @@ jobs:
           # Regression guard for issue #45 — see the Linux/Windows job for rationale.
           for bin in dist/cass-memory-macos-arm64 dist/cass-memory-macos-x64; do
             echo "Scanning $bin for mangled trauma-guard emoji..."
-            if strings "$bin" | grep -E '\\u\{[0-9a-fA-F]+\} ?HOT STOVE|HOT STOVE ?\\u\{[0-9a-fA-F]+\}' | head -5; then
+            matches="$(strings "$bin" | grep -E '\\u\{[0-9a-fA-F]+\} ?HOT STOVE|HOT STOVE ?\\u\{[0-9a-fA-F]+\}' | head -5 || true)"
+            if [ -n "$matches" ]; then
+              echo "$matches"
               echo "::error::Binary $bin contains Rust-style \\u{...} escapes in trauma-guard text (regression of #45)."
               echo "::error::Route the character through \${VAR} interpolation. See src/trauma_guard_script.ts."
               exit 1
